headline: New in Deep Agents v0.6
subject: 🚨 AI Alert: 5 new launches — LangChain Blog

article_items.title: New in Deep Agents v0.6
article_items.description: Deep Agents 0.6 ships a code interpreter, harness profiles, streaming v3, delta channels, and ContextHub, making agents faster, cheaper, and more scalable.
article_items.excerpt: Deep Agents 0.6 ships a code interpreter, harness profiles, streaming v3, delta channels, and ContextHub, making agents faster, cheaper, and more scalable.

article_items.title: Everything we shipped at Interrupt
article_items.description: From autonomous debugging to one-line deploys, here's every product LangChain launched at Interrupt 2026 to help teams build, test, and ship agents in production.
article_items.excerpt: From autonomous debugging to one-line deploys, here's every product LangChain launched at Interrupt 2026 to help teams build, test, and ship agents in production.

article_items.title: Tuning Deep Agents to Work Well with Different Models
article_items.description: Deep Agents was previously designed in a generic way to work well across model families. Today we’re adding model-specific profiles to adjust prompts, tools, and middleware. We ship profiles for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models out of the box, which we see leads to a 10–20 point jump on a subset of tau2-bench over the default harness.
article_items.excerpt: Deep Agents was previously designed in a generic way to work well across model families. Today we’re adding model-specific profiles to adjust prompts, tools, and middleware. We ship profiles for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google models out of the box, which we see leads to a 10–20 point jump on a subset of tau2-bench over the default harness.

article_items.title: Microsoft Edge just stopped storing your passwords in plaintext - but you'll need the latest update
article_items.description: Though Microsoft claimed that storing plaintext passwords in memory was by design, the company has changed the behavior to better protect your passwords.
article_items.excerpt: Though Microsoft claimed that storing plaintext passwords in memory was by design, the company has changed the behavior to better protect your passwords.

article_items.title: Build custom code-based evaluators in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: In this post, you will implement four Lambda-based custom code evaluators for a financial market-intelligence agent, register each with AgentCore, and run them in on-demand and online modes. You will also see how to combine custom code-based evaluators with built-in evaluators and how to call other AWS services for grounded fact-checking, PII detection, and real-time alerting.
article_items.excerpt: In this post, you will implement four Lambda-based custom code evaluators for a financial market-intelligence agent, register each with AgentCore, and run them in on-demand and online modes. You will also see how to combine custom code-based evaluators with built-in evaluators and how to call other AWS services for grounded fact-checking, PII detection, and real-time alerting.
